title: Oven Lemon Pepper Chicken with Potatoes and Broccoli
description: One-pan lemon pepper chicken breasts with steam-roasted potatoes and broccoli.
ingredients: 4 medium Yukon Gold or red potatoes (~600g), cut into 1-inch chunks
1.5 tbsp extra virgin olive oil (for potatoes)
2 tbsp extra virgin olive oil (for chicken glaze)
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F. Line a large sheet pan with foil and coat with cooking spray.

Toss potato chunks with 1.5 tbsp olive oil and 1.5 tsp kosher salt. Spread on the sheet pan and add 1/2 cup water directly to the pan. Roast at 450°F for 20–25 minutes, stirring once halfway, until water has steamed off and potatoes are beginning to brown.

While potatoes roast, whisk together 2 tbsp olive oil, 3 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tbsp maple syrup, 2 tsp lemon pepper seasoning, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp onion powder, 1/2 tsp smoked paprika, and 1/4 tsp salt. Coat 4 chicken breasts thoroughly in the glaze.
Drop oven to 425°F. Push potatoes to the sides of the pan and add chicken breasts to the center.
Roast for 10 minutes, then add 4 cups broccoli florets around the chicken. Continue roasting 10–15 more minutes until chicken reaches 165°F internal temp and broccoli is lightly charred.
Rest chicken 5 minutes before slicing. Spoon pan juices over everything before serving.
notes: Two-stage heat is the key — 450°F with water drives the steam roast on the potatoes, 425°F finishes the chicken without drying it out. Broccoli goes in last so it roasts rather than steams. Use two sheet pans if needed to avoid crowding, which kills the roast on both the potatoes and broccoli.
